title.en: Ensure nodev option set on /tmp partition

prose.en: Ensure nodev option set on /tmp partition

description.en: The `nodev` mount option specifies that the filesystem cannot contain special devices.

rationale.en: Since the `/tmp` filesystem is not intended to support devices, set this option to ensure that users cannot create a block or character special devices in `/tmp`.

audit.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/tmp`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nodev` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/tmp` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/tmp` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /tmp
```

```
 /tmp         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

remediation.en:
**\- IF \-** a separate partition exists for `/tmp`.  

Edit the `/etc/fstab` file and add `nodev` to the fourth field (mounting options) for the `/tmp` partition.  

*Example:*  

Run the following command to remount `/tmp` with the configured options:

```
# mount -o remount /tmp
```

```
 /tmp         defaults,rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime  0 0
```

references: See the fstab(5) manual page for more information.
